content.plain_english: This is a recap week. Your child is going back over what they have learned so far — counting, simple addition and subtraction, and shapes — and trying it out on mixed problems.
content.in_school: Lessons mix old favourites — counting cubes, drawing number lines, sorting shapes — with short reasoning tasks. Teachers gently spot any small wobbles and revisit the parts children find tricky. There is no testing.
content.home_activity: Pick up an old maths book or any sheet your child has brought home this term. Choose one page and ask them to walk you through it again — what was the maths? What is easier now? Five minutes of quiet revisiting, no pressure.
dinner_table_questions: What is one thing you find easy in maths now that felt tricky in September?
Can you teach me one thing you've learned this term?
Which is your favourite maths game and why?

key_vocabulary.definition: Look back at something you have learned.
Use words to describe how you worked something out.
Something that repeats in a regular order.
A set of things that go together.
Look at two things to see how they are the same or different.

example_questions: Show me 7 in three different ways.
How many 2-D shapes can you name?
content.times_table_tip: Year 1 children practise counting in 2s, 5s and 10s. Pick one of those — say the 5s — and count up to 50 once a day for a week.
